proposed_from.topic: Sharp critical-completeness threshold for proximal identification of long-term ATE via outcome-bridge functions when the completeness condition on negative-control proxies approaches violation
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue prior art is the downgraded eid_proximal_phase_v1 proposal, which had a finite null-annihilator/static bridge-envelope diagnostic but was rejected as a finite taxonomy rather than a generic-class theorem. This v5 proposal keeps that algebra only as Theorem 1 scaffolding and makes the novel kernel the local triangular-array rho_n certificate and residual projection frontier in Conjectures 1--2.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 for nonunique bridges, BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 for strongly identified functionals, SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025 for integral-equation confidence sets, AndrewsCheng2012 and AntoineLavergne2014 for weak/semi-strong identification, plus Santos2012 and Hong2017 for partial-ID conditional moment inference. None gives a finite proximal negative-control target-loading certificate rho_n, its three-region iff frontier, or the bridge-envelope projection endpoint."}
seed_list: Construct the target-active null score alpha_LT(P) for long-term proximal ATE and conjecture it is the sharp iff frontier between nonunique-bridge point ID and a non-singleton bridge envelope.

Prove a common-kernel bridge-compatibility threshold for two-sample long-term ATE: early proxy panels identify the terminal ATE iff the experimental loading lies in the observational bridge row space.
Give a finite observable tau_native(P) criterion under which proximal OR/DR residual bridge selectors and minimax bridge selectors induce the same ATE functional at the completeness boundary.

Prove a finite bridge-envelope realization theorem: every bounded observable proximal outcome-bridge endpoint is induced by an actual latent negative-control mean law, or characterize the counterexample class.
Develop a local-to-incomplete weak-proxy phase diagram for proximal ATE estimators when target-relevant singular values shrink at n^{-1/2} scale.
Refine Miao-Geng-Tchetgen categorical proxy rank ID by replacing full proxy rank with a target-specific row-space rank condition for ATE-active proxy contrasts.
Extend semiparametric proximal DR inference by defining a source-condition double-robust completeness threshold for long-term ATE regularity.
Bridge Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen proxy partial-ID bounds and proximal point ID through a non-LP Tikhonov bridge-envelope representation.
Replace untestable completeness pretesting with a Canay-Santos-Shaikh-style sensitivity report indexed by alpha_LT(P) and a bridge residual radius.
For surrogate-index long-term ATE, characterize when multiple short-term proxies span the proximal bridge loading and when residual drift forces sharp partial ID.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the target-active null score alpha_LT(P) for long-term proximal ATE and conjecture it is the sharp iff frontier between nonunique-bridge point ID and a non-singleton bridge envelope.

anchor_paper.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Sections 2-4 use rank/completeness for proxy identification and do not characterize a target-specific failure boundary.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens completeness, but nontrivial because it isolates target-active null directions and predicts an open-class point/interval boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 or Bennett et al. because functionals constant on bridge fibers are already characterized.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those papers do not define the long-term two-sample ATE loading alpha_LT(P), bridge-envelope realization clause, or local critical-completeness width.

adversarial_referee.instance: The score may be just generic row-space algebra.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch is the named long-term loading, open-class bridge-envelope realization, and local weak-completeness phase diagram.
Necessity is stated through non-singleton Theta_I(P) under bounded relative-interior slack.

adversarial_referee.instance: Open-class claim could rely on a rank-zero toy matrix.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal includes a binary K_rho active-singular witness with interior means, not Y constant.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a common-kernel bridge-compatibility threshold for two-sample long-term ATE: early proxy panels identify the terminal ATE iff the experimental loading lies in the observational bridge row space.

anchor_paper.author: Guido Imbens, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Yuhao Wang
anchor_paper.claim: Long-term effects are identified through sufficient sequential/proxy structure rather than a sharp proxy-richness frontier.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends long-term proxy ID, but nontrivial because the common-kernel condition couples two samples rather than adding another surrogate assumption.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the failed pid_long_term_surrogate common-kernel suggestion restated.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets an exact bridge-row-space iff condition, not prevalence dilation or generic LP containment.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sufficiency is easier than the necessity counterexample.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Necessity would use two observationally equivalent laws differing only along the experimental loading.

adversarial_referee.instance: Imbens et al. may already impose an equivalent bridge condition.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Their condition is sufficient sequential/proxy structure, not the exact row-space threshold.

adversarial_referee.instance: ATE only; ATT or PRTE may follow from the same bridge.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Could be promoted by defining a family of target loadings c_tau and row-space checks jointly.

seed_details.one_line: Give a finite observable tau_native(P) criterion under which proximal OR/DR residual bridge selectors and minimax bridge selectors induce the same ATE functional at the completeness boundary.

anchor_paper.author: Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Masatoshi Uehara
anchor_paper.claim: Minimax bridge learning relaxes uniqueness/completeness reliance but lacks finite observable equivalence thresholds against OR/DR residual selectors.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Equivalence frontier; nontrivial only if it compares named OR/DR and minimax objectives through KKT conditions.
seed_details.referee_objection: The archived eid_proximal_phase proposal already pursued tau_native(P).
seed_details.rebuttal: It is viable only if recast with exact population quadratic criteria and generic selector-range theorem; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: OR/DR residual selector may not target the same object as minimax.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Need explicit population criteria for both before this seed is selected.

adversarial_referee.instance: Prior proposal already exhausted this angle.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: This is a pivot candidate only if structurally sharpened beyond the banked tau_native run.

adversarial_referee.instance: No second comparator chain beyond Kallus minimax.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Comparator chain uses Cui OR/DR residual and Mastouri kernel moment bridges.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a finite bridge-envelope realization theorem: every bounded observable proximal outcome-bridge endpoint is induced by an actual latent negative-control mean law, or characterize the counterexample class.

anchor_paper.author: prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewers flagged that observable bridge-fiber extrema were treated as causally realizable without proving the hard endpoint attainability step.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness-failure; nontrivial because it attacks a named recurring CausalSmith sharpness gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a finite response-type LP realization theorem like Balke-Pearl.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proximal bridge equations and negative-control exclusions create a different observable bridge-array completion problem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Counterexample may use an impossible or collapsed observed law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require positive-cell finite law and bounded nonconstant outcomes.

adversarial_referee.instance: May lack a named published comparator.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use Ghassami proxy partial ID and Balke-Pearl finite sharpness as comparators.

adversarial_referee.instance: ATE realization only may not cover bridge endpoints jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State realization for the whole bridge-envelope support function.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a local-to-incomplete weak-proxy phase diagram for proximal ATE estimators when target-relevant singular values shrink at n^{-1/2} scale.

anchor_paper.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Nonunique bridges can still identify counterfactual means, but weak-completeness asymptotics as the operator approaches nonidentification are not developed.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends inverse-problem asymptotics to proximal; nontrivial because the target-active singular coefficient determines point/weak/partial-ID phases.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from NPIV weak identification theory.
seed_details.rebuttal: NPIV theory lacks the proximal two-bridge causal loading and bridge-envelope partial-ID endpoint.

adversarial_referee.instance: No exact identification theorem if it is only inference.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Pair with alpha_LT(P) exact-ID frontier as the identification object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Bennett et al. may subsume strong functional inference.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use their result as comparator; novelty is local phase diagram to failed completeness.

adversarial_referee.instance: Weak threshold could have unconstrained sigma.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Define omega_n=sqrt(n) sigma_target/|a_target| with bounded slack and singular-gap assumptions.

seed_details.one_line: Refine Miao-Geng-Tchetgen categorical proxy rank ID by replacing full proxy rank with a target-specific row-space rank condition for ATE-active proxy contrasts.

anchor_paper.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Categorical proxy identification is framed through rank conditions rather than target-specific row-space rank.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens rank; stronger because failure can be target-inactive or target-active, producing a phase boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is linear algebra already implicit in the paper's matrix equations.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone seed it may be too incremental unless tied to long-term loading and bridge-envelope width.

seed_details.one_line: Extend semiparametric proximal DR inference by defining a source-condition double-robust completeness threshold for long-term ATE regularity.

anchor_paper.author: Yifan Cui, Hongming Pu, Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Semiparametric proximal inference depends on bridge equations and regularity, without a finite weak-bridge selector frontier.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends estimator theory; nontrivial only if the threshold changes the efficient influence function or regularity class.
seed_details.referee_objection: Bennett et al. already solve strongly identified functionals of weakly identified functions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Could survive only as a proximal DR source-condition comparator, not the chosen exact-ID kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Bridge Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen proxy partial-ID bounds and proximal point ID through a non-LP Tikhonov bridge-envelope representation.

anchor_paper.author: AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Partial-identification bounds are proposed when completeness fails and bridge or causal-effect point identification is unavailable.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Non-LP representation; nontrivial if it yields an evaluable Tikhonov envelope rather than an LP restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This may just repackage proxy partial-ID bounds in inverse-problem notation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Needs a closed-form spectral envelope or support-function dual to avoid being merely notational.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims non-LP but may deliver finite LP endpoints.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require Tikhonov/spectral support-function representation as the focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Tikhonov envelope may only contain the sharp set.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Sharpness must be stated as equality or downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Ghassami bounds may not use bridge functions directly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State as bridge between no-completeness proxy bounds and bridge-fiber point ID.

seed_details.one_line: Replace untestable completeness pretesting with a Canay-Santos-Shaikh-style sensitivity report indexed by alpha_LT(P) and a bridge residual radius.

anchor_paper.author: Ivan Canay, Andres Santos, Azeem Shaikh
anchor_paper.claim: Completeness-type identification hypotheses can be empirically untestable in a strong sense.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity to assumption violation; nontrivial only with closed-form critical radius where point ID collapses.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a recommendation, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Would need L(delta), U(delta), and delta*; otherwise not selected.

seed_details.one_line: For surrogate-index long-term ATE, characterize when multiple short-term proxies span the proximal bridge loading and when residual drift forces sharp partial ID.

anchor_paper.author: Susan Athey, Raj Chetty, Guido Imbens, Hyunseung Kang
anchor_paper.claim: Surrogate-index ATE is identified under Prentice surrogacy, with bias analysis rather than proximal completeness thresholds.
anchor_paper.location: NBER w26463 abstract and surrogacy assumption

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends surrogate index; nontrivial if the proxy span threshold gives an iff frontier, weak otherwise.
seed_details.referee_objection: The failed long-term-surrogate bank already exhausted noisy-panel drift bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only viable if phrased as proximal bridge-loading span, not prevalence dilation.

proposed_from.literature_map: 1. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika, MiaoGengTchetgen2018) identify causal effects with proxy variables under rank/completeness conditions and turn to testing when proxy information is insufficient. 2. Cui, Pu, Shi, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, JASA, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024) develop semiparametric proximal inference and proximal double robustness under bridge equations. 3. Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, Statistics and Probability Letters, ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023) show counterfactual means may remain identified without unique bridge functions. 4. Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, arXiv, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023) develop proxy partial-identification bounds without completeness. 5. Canay, Santos, and Shaikh (2013, Econometrica, CanaySantosShaikh2013) show completeness-type identification hypotheses can be empirically untestable. 6. Bennett et al. (2023, COLT/PMLR, BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023) study inference on strongly identified functionals of weakly identified nuisance functions. 7. Carrasco, Florens, and Renault (2007) and Chen and Reiss (2011) provide inverse-problem and singular-value regularization language. 8. Kallus, Mao, and Uehara (2021) and Mastouri et al. (2021) study minimax/kernel bridge learning without finite selector-equivalence thresholds. 9. Athey, Chetty, Imbens, and Kang (2019) and Imbens, Kallus, Mao, and Wang (2025) motivate long-term ATE identification from short-term proxies, but not a sharp proximal completeness frontier. 10. The active gap is between exact completeness point ID and no-completeness partial ID: a target-specific null-space threshold for long-term proximal ATE.

banked_reason: Stage -1.2 NO-PASS @ flagship (D-1.2 effort=high). 25 reviewer rounds across 4 angles; final angle=4 v5 REVISE@flagship S=0 N=0 C=1. Never advanced past D-0.5. Topic reached flagship shape but the named focal scalar (critical completeness) is an operator-theoretic limit (minimum singular value of an unobserved integral operator), not evaluable in observed-data primitives — recurring C-flag on usability/evaluability of the threshold. Anti-pattern: 'Computable map absent' satisfied in name but not in evaluability. High-effort drafter could not rescue an operator-spectrum focal object.
